Phase-80.NAME: Manual External MTProto Proxy Pool and Delivery Mode
Phase-80.objective: Add an administrator-controlled fallback mode that delivers manually verified external MTProto proxies to user cabinets while preserving the existing automatic KrotPN MTProto implementation.

scope.in-scope: Manual external proxy storage, encrypted secret handling, admin CRUD and activation UI, delivery-mode switch, owner endpoint payload selection, user cabinet display, audit logging, redaction, and tests.
scope.out-of-scope: Changing deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, VPN/AWG runtime, KPprotoN runtime, SNI router topology, automatic KrotPN MTProto assignment generation, provider-level DPI bypass engineering, or collecting telemetry from external proxies.

requirements.requirement-1: Administrator can add one or more external MTProto proxies with server, port, secret, name, priority, status, and notes.
requirements.requirement-2: Administrator can switch delivery mode from automatic to manual_external and back without reissuing existing automatic KrotPN assignments.
requirements.requirement-3: When manual_external mode is active, authenticated users see the active external proxy in the same Telegram proxy card flow.
requirements.requirement-4: Manual external proxy secrets are encrypted at rest, redacted from admin lists/logs/audit, and exposed only through authenticated owner delivery.
requirements.requirement-5: Admin UI must state that external proxy statistics, IP history, runtime health, abuse analytics, and promotion-tag application are unavailable from KrotPN.
requirements.requirement-6: Automatic KrotPN MTProto mode must remain available and unchanged for future return to the internal runtime.

architecture.flow-1: M-082 stores manual external proxy rows and a singleton delivery setting through additive Alembic schema changes.
architecture.flow-2: M-006/M-047 expose admin-only routes for listing, creating, updating, activating, disabling, and switching delivery mode.
architecture.flow-3: M-026 records audit-safe admin actions without full secrets or full Telegram links.
architecture.flow-4: M-045 owner endpoint calls M-082 selection logic before returning the user MTProto payload: automatic mode delegates to M-043; manual_external returns the active manual proxy.
architecture.flow-5: M-009/M-075 render manual external proxy data in the existing compact Telegram MTProto card with source and telemetry-unavailable copy.
architecture.flow-6: M-010/M-076 render compact admin controls inside MTProto operations/settings without exposing full secrets in tables.
architecture.flow-7: M-058/M-059 remain honest: analytics and promotion-tag controls apply only to KrotPN-managed runtime proxies, not external manual proxies.

planned-interfaces.api-1: GET /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies returns paginated redacted manual proxy summaries.
planned-interfaces.api-2: POST /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies creates an encrypted external proxy row after validation.
planned-interfaces.api-3: PATCH /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies/{proxy_id} updates name/server/port/status/notes or replaces the secret without returning the old secret.
planned-interfaces.api-4: POST /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies/{proxy_id}/activate requires confirmation and selects one active manual proxy.
planned-interfaces.api-5: POST /api/v1/admin/mtproto/manual-proxies/{proxy_id}/disable requires confirmation and removes a proxy from manual delivery.
planned-interfaces.api-6: GET /api/v1/admin/mtproto/delivery-mode returns automatic/manual_external state and active manual proxy summary.
planned-interfaces.api-7: PUT /api/v1/admin/mtproto/delivery-mode requires confirmation and switches delivery mode.
planned-interfaces.api-8: GET /api/v1/mtproto/proxy returns source=krotpn_auto or source=manual_external plus telemetry_available=false for manual external delivery.

steps.step-1: Map current M-043/M-045 owner issuance and M-047/M-058 admin MTProto surfaces so the delivery selector changes only the approved response path.
steps.step-2: Add additive database schema for manual external proxies and singleton delivery settings with encrypted secret storage and safe default automatic mode.
steps.step-3: Implement backend validation for host, port, hex secret, status, priority, notes, and generated tg:// plus https://t.me links.
steps.step-4: Implement admin API for proxy pool CRUD, activate/disable, delivery-mode get/set, redaction, and confirmation guards.
steps.step-5: Implement audit logging for create/update/activate/disable/mode-switch without secret or full link leakage.
steps.step-6: Implement owner payload selection so automatic mode remains current behavior and manual_external mode returns the active external proxy with telemetry_available=false.
steps.step-7: Update user frontend API types and Telegram MTProto card to show manual external proxy links compactly and honestly.
steps.step-8: Update admin frontend MTProto page with a compact delivery-mode control, proxy pool list, add/edit form, active badge, and warnings about unavailable telemetry.
steps.step-9: Add backend tests for validation, encryption/redaction, automatic-mode preservation, manual-mode owner response, missing-active-proxy fallback, and confirmation guards.
steps.step-10: Add frontend/admin smoke tests for UI markers, masked secrets, source labels, compact layout, and no false telemetry promises.
steps.step-11: Run frontend and frontend-admin builds plus targeted backend MTProto/admin tests.
steps.step-12: Run XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose/VPN-runtime/MTProto-runtime guard.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: Manual fallback: admin adds a verified external proxy, activates it, switches delivery mode to manual_external, and a user opening /dashboard receives the external proxy link without touching KrotPN automatic assignments.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Return to automatic: admin switches delivery mode back to automatic, the owner endpoint resumes M-043 issuance/reuse, and previously issued automatic assignments remain preserved.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: No active manual proxy: admin selects manual_external without an active proxy or disables the active proxy; users see a safe preparing/degraded message, not a broken empty link.

risks.risk-1: External proxy may stop working outside KrotPN control; mitigate with explicit operator verification and clear UI labeling.
risks.risk-2: Shared manual proxy can be overloaded or blocked for all users; mitigate with pool support and fast admin activation of another verified proxy.
risks.risk-3: Secret leakage risk rises when admin stores third-party credentials; mitigate with encryption, redaction, no logs, and owner-only full delivery.
risks.risk-4: Admin may expect analytics from external proxies; mitigate by marking telemetry unavailable everywhere this mode is shown.
risks.risk-5: Delivery selector could accidentally reissue automatic assignments; mitigate with tests proving automatic assignment identity is preserved across mode switches.

acceptance.item-1: Admin can configure and activate a manual external MTProto proxy without changing deploy/runtime topology.
acceptance.item-2: User cabinet receives working-format Telegram links from the manual external proxy when manual_external mode is active.
acceptance.item-3: Automatic KrotPN MTProto mode remains unchanged and can be restored by switching the delivery mode back.
acceptance.item-4: Secrets are encrypted/redacted and no admin list, audit record, log marker, or smoke artifact leaks full manual proxy credentials.
acceptance.item-5: UI clearly states that external proxy telemetry/statistics/abuse analytics/promo-tag application are unavailable from KrotPN.
